chapter: Reverence towards the Sanctity of the Muslims.
arabic: عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَثَلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي تَوَادِّهِمْ وَتَرَاحُمِهِمْ وَتَعَاطُفِهِمْ، مَثَلُ الْجَسَدِ إِذَا اشْتَكَى مِنْهُ عُضْوٌ تَدَاعَى لَهُ سَائرُ الْجَسَدِ بِالسَّهَرِ وَالْحُمَّى».
english: Narrated Nu'man bin Bashir: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said, 'The believers in their mutual kindness, compassion, and sympathy are just like one body. When one of the limbs suffers, the whole body responds to it with wakefulness and fever.'
explanation: This foundational narration establishes the global spiritual and moral unity of the Muslim community, dictating that empathy and concern cannot be limited by geography, race, or tribal lines.
